data.title: Rivian (RIVN) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Here's What to Expect
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 after the market closes on Thursday, February 12. Rivian bulls are upbeat about the launch of the electric vehicle (EV) maker's R2 sport-utility vehicle (SUV) this year and recent updates on its new in-house chip and Autonomy+ subscription service. However, other analysts are concerned about the lack of profitability, the end of the $7,500 EV tax credits, and a slowdown in EV demand.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Rivian to report a loss per share of $0.71 for Q4 2025. Revenue is projected to decline by more than 27% to $1.26 billion. The decline in Q4 topline reflects a 31% drop in deliveries to 9,745 vehicles as demand was pulled forward into the third quarter ahead of the end of the $7,500 EV tax credit.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise as Investors Assess Jobs Data, Key Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended higher on Thursday morning following a stronger-than-expected January jobs report. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.22%, 0.24%, and 0.19%, respectively, at 8:20 a.m. EST on February 12.

data.title: Memory Stocks Rally as Kioxia Sees Customers Locking In Supply Through 2028
data.description:
Memory chip stocks moved higher today after Japanese chipmaker Kioxia (KXHCF) delivered strong full-year guidance. The company, which focuses on NAND flash memory, posted sales and income targets that came in above analyst views. As a result, investors saw fresh signs that demand is staying firm across the memory space.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, Kioxia shared new details on customer behavior that stood out. Management noted that while deals are usually signed one year out, some buyers now want to secure supply for 2027 and 2028. That suggests the current supply gap may last longer than many had expected.

The company also pointed to rising prices. CFO Hideki Hanazawa said the market is seeing "a very sharp increase in selling price," which supports the idea that memory makers may regain pricing power as AI demand grows.

Key U.S. Memory Names Trade Higher

Following the update, several major memory stocks jumped in premarket trading. SanDisk Corporation (SNDK) rose the most after news tied to its joint venture work with Kioxia. Micron Technology (MU) also climbed, while Western Digital Corporation (WDC) and Seagate Technology Holdings plc (STX) moved higher as well.

Moreover, the broader backdrop remains tied to AI growth. Micron recently said shipments of next-gen high-bandwidth chips have started ahead of schedule. Now, Kioxia's outlook adds another signal that demand for memory tied to AI systems may remain strong into the years ahead.

By using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we've assembled all the memory chip stocks appearing in the piece to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader chip industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Bitcoin Shrugs Off 'Extreme Fear' as Blockbuster Jobs Report Fails to Break the $67,000 Support
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is defying the standard macro rulebook today, as the asset holds steady near $67,800 despite a surge in U.S. employment data. While a hot jobs report typically sends risk assets tumbling by pushing back interest rate cuts, the market is currently viewing the numbers with a more nuanced lens. Investors are focusing on the fact that while the headline number surged, the actual growth was concentrated in specific sectors. Moreover, the muted price reaction suggests that the aggressive selling pressure of the past month may finally be reaching exhaustion.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Macro Data Creates a Mixed Signal

The U.S. economy added 130,000 jobs in January, nearly doubling the consensus estimate of 70,000. This data has effectively forced traders to price out a Federal Reserve rate cut until at least July; however, the report also revealed that hiring was largely limited to healthcare and government sectors. This hidden cooling in the broader economy is providing a cushion for Bitcoin, as it suggests the Fed may still have room to move if inflation continues to trend downward.

BlackRock Builds a DeFi Bridge with Uniswap

While Bitcoin consolidates, the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector is erupting following a landmark move by BlackRock (BLK). The world's largest asset manager is listing its $2.2 billion BUIDL fund, a tokenized U.S. Treasury product, directly on Uniswap (UNI-USD). Robert Mitchnick, BlackRock's Global Head of Digital Assets, described the move as a "major leap forward in the interoperability of tokenized USD yield funds with stablecoins." BlackRock also disclosed a strategic stake in the UNI governance token; consequently, UNI surged as much as 25% to reach $4.11 before stabilizing near $3.35.

Crypto Sentiment Hits a Post-FTX Low

The technical backdrop remains a challenge since the Crypto Fear & Greed Index has bottomed out at a reading of 5, a level of sustained pessimism not seen since 2022. Traders are currently paying a panic premium for downside protection, with put options accounting for 54% of daily volume. Despite this atmosphere of extreme caution, the $68,800 level has emerged as a core liquidation zone; specifically, if Bitcoin can break above this resistance, it could trigger a short squeeze that forces the market out of its extreme fear phase.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $67,910.51.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Is NovoCure Stock (NVCR) Up Today?
data.description:
NovoCure (NVCR) stock rocketed higher on Thursday after the oncology company received approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for Optune Pax. Optune Pax is a treatment for adult patients with locally advanced pancreatic cancer in combination with cancer treatments gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The FDA's approval of Optune Pax as a treatment for locally advanced pancreatic cancer followed strong Phase 3 trial results. The company's PANOVA-3 trial included 571 patients who were randomized 1:1 and followed for a minimum of 18 months. It met its primary endpoint, which was a statistically significant improvement in median overall survival for patients treated with Optune Pax.

Frank Leonard, CEO of Novocure, said, "The FDA approval of Optune Pax marks the first new treatment in decades for people living with locally advanced pancreatic cancer. Systemic therapies have shown poor bioavailability in pancreatic tumors, limiting their effectiveness. Optune Pax is a fundamentally different treatment, utilizing a biophysical approach that targets the unique electrical properties of cancer cells. This is a proud moment for Novocure and we look forward to bringing Optune Pax to patients and the healthcare providers who care for them."

NovoCure Stock Movement Today

NovoCure stock was up 29.33% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a slight gain yesterday. However, the shares have fallen 18.79% year-to-date and 51.64% over the past 12 months.

NVCR stock trading activity this morning saw some 399,000 shares change hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 1.27 million units.
data.author: William White

data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase (COIN) is set to report its Q4 earnings results on Thursday, February 12, and the options market is expecting a volatile reaction. data.title: Why D-Wave (QBTS) Stock Is Up Today and What TD Cowen Sees Ahead
data.description:
D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) stock traded higher early Thursday after top TD Cowen analyst Krish Sankar initiated coverage on the stock with a Buy rating. The 5-star analyst said D-Wave already earns real revenue, which sets it apart from many quantum companies still in the research stage. He said continued demand for the company's Advantage system sales and cloud usage should support growth in the coming years.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Sankar Is Bullish on QBTS Stock

Sankar said D-Wave's technology is already being used for practical tasks like scheduling and logistics. As more companies test quantum tools, he expects adoption to expand.

TD Cowen projects revenue of about $26 million for CY25, rising to roughly $135 million by 2028, implying more than 70% annual growth. Enterprise systems can also sell for $20 million to $40 million per unit with high margins.

Sankar also pointed to D-Wave's recent acquisition of Quantum Circuit Inc. technology, which could speed development of future processors and open the door to AI and machine-learning workloads later this decade.

Sankar ranks #10 out of 12,108 analysts tracked on TipRanks. He has a 66% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 41.90%.

What Could Drive the Stock

The analyst said D-Wave has a clearer path to making money than many quantum peers because companies can already use its systems and cloud service for real tasks. He also noted the company has enough cash to pursue deals and roll out new products faster.

However, Sankar warned that risks remain, including production hurdles and rising competition from other quantum technologies.

Overall, the analyst views D-Wave as an early commercial leader in quantum computing, already generating revenue with greater potential ahead as new systems roll out.

Is QBTS Stock a Good Stock to Invest In? 

Analysts remain highly optimistic about D-Wave's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, QBTS stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 12 unanimous Buys. The average D-Wave Quantum price target of $41.25 implies 110% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Meta Stock Up on $10B Indiana Buildout despite $660B Big Tech AI Spend Worries
data.description:
Shares in Meta (META) edged higher early Wednesday after the U.S. tech giant pressed on with its aggressive data center expansion by breaking ground on its new facility in Lebanon, Indiana. The California-based company noted that the data center is worth more than $10 billion.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The development comes as Meta, which broke ground on its 30th data center in Wisconsin in November, plans to devote the bulk of its up to $169 billion in 2026 expenses to AI infrastructure and related initiatives. Plans by tech supermajors such as Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta to spend up to $660 billion on AI this year have emerged as a key source of concern for investors, especially due to shrinking cash flow.

Meta has said the new site will deliver one gigawatt of power and will be designed to meet the demand of both the company's AI workloads and those of its core products.

"As AI advances and compute demands continue to grow, gigawatt sites like this one will be critical to advancing the technology that supports our core business as well as our AI ambitions," Meta noted in a blog post.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/12/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise after Strong Jobs Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed early Thursday following a stronger-than-expected January jobs report. Payrolls rose by 130,000, and the unemployment rate fell to 4.3%, easing concerns about the labor market. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.24%, 0.28%, and 0.26%, respectively, at 6:10 a.m. EST on February 12.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, all three indexes closed lower, with the Dow falling 0.13%, the S&P 500 dipping around 0.1%, and the Nasdaq down 0.16%.

After markets closed, Cisco Systems (CSCO) fell 7% despite beating Q2 expectations, due to weak guidance. Meanwhile, McDonald's (MCD) rose after surpassing earnings estimates.

Turning toward the economic calendar, data on initial jobless claims will be released.

On the earnings front, notable companies including Coinbase Global (COIN), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Roku (ROKU), and DraftKings (DKNG) will release earnings today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.17%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $64.36 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,061 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today amid positive corporate earnings.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on February 12. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index dropped 0.86%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.05%, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.80%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 0.02%, but the Topix increased 0.70% as part of the post-election rally.

data.title: Nebius Earnings: NBIS Stock Falls as Q4 Miss Disappoints Investors
data.description:
Shares of Nebius Group (NBIS) are falling in pre-market trading after the company reported its Q4 2025 results, which missed Wall Street estimates. In the three months ended December 31, 2025,  revenue came in at $227.7 million, below the consensus estimate of $247.5 million. Meanwhile, adjusted EBITDA totaled $15 million, also falling short of expectations of $22.55 million.
